-- 3 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 03
The year is 2015. T-shirt sellers are selling high
volumes of personalised tees via Facebook ads. I
spot an opportunity for the print-on-demand
children’s book company I’ve just scaled from
$800K to $8M in less than a year; “I’ve built this
tool to take a child’s name and embed it into an
image on the fly!” said my developer. Lightbulb!
There were around 100,000 unique names in our
database - we simply needed to code the name
and generate 100,000 images so I could use these
in retargeting ads using a Dynamic Catalog.
We developed a pretty cool solution but, since we
were spending around £25K per day on ads, our
Facebook rep, on seeing the solution, suggested
we passed it to their Policy team to ensure it
passed compliance.
This was the very first time I had any interaction
with them.
“Sorry, these ads are not policy compliant”.
What?!
Facebook advised that as they used personal
information, even if it was collected from their input
on our website, they didn’t explicitly consent to us
using it. So we had to scrap our fancy new tool.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Whether You Like It Or Not,
Your Compliance Is Necessary
I hated policy compliance at that point. And it’s only
become harder and harder to launch policy
compliant ad campaigns with Facebook. I would not
be surprised if 100% of Facebook advertisers have
all been hit with a disapproved ad at some point.
We put this together to help you get a better start
and to continue on the right path with your
Facebook ad campaigns.
We’ve added a single page checklist below for
reference to print and use as a launch guide, or to
share with your team to build your regular
processes.
Further below we’ve shared additional information
on what we do for each checklist.
You may not need all of it, every single time, but
there’s no harm in ensuring you and your team are
aware of the most important policy checks we like
to focus on.
To your success,
Depesh Mandalia
CEO at SM Commerce.
Depesh
www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 04
Ads Policy Launch Checklist
A handy checklist to keep with you for your campaign launches.
Business Manager Checklist
Create two Business Managers
Activate 2-Factor Authentication (2FA)
Remove unnecessary users
Assign at least 2 admins
Ad Accounts Checklist
Setup billing for auto payment
Backup ad account and remove unnecessary Ad Accounts
Appeal or delete existing disapproved ads
Account Quality checks
Ads Checklist
Be truthful and clear in your copy
Avoid tricking users to click your ad
If your ads use a lot of ‘skin’ be careful about how you pitch it
Avoid calling out personal attributes
Ask yourself if your targeting could be considered “discriminatory”
Page Checklist
Remove your profile from unnecessary Pages/Accounts
Maintain a high Page post engagement rate
Maintain a high post positive feedback score
Maintain Monthly Feedback feedback score above 3
Ensure Page adheres to general content guidelines
Personal Profile
Ensure login details are kept private
Check-in when travelling
Stop using your personal profile for business purposes
Enable 2 factor authentication for your personal profile
Landing Pages
Maintain landing page load times under 3 seconds
Ensure content theme closely matches ad themes
Ensure landing pages have standard footer links
Verify your URL and trust rating
Maintain landing page functionality and quality
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•	www.getwsodo.com

Ok let’s start with the basics. Is your business model
compliant with Facebook advertising? If your product falls
into certain niches you’ll automatically be disapproved, like
gambling and adult categories.
The second most important thing Facebook looks for is the
impact on the user. Without users and without happy users,
there is no Facebook. There’s a huge clue in that in terms of
how to approach your advertising campaigns too.
Let’s now go through our checklist ensure everything is setup
correctly.
You can print and check off the tasks that are completed.
Facebook’s core principles
and rules
www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 06
Facebook’s Business Manager (BM) is the
only way of properly managing your ad
account, containing your ad account, ad pixel,
Page and other assets you might choose to
use along the way.
Needless to say (though I’ll say it anyway),
you don’t want to lose your BM!
Mistakes happen and Facebook can disable
your BM accidentally. If you’re confident it
was a mistake, appeal as soon as you can.
If you’re asking ‘how will I know if my BM is
banned?’ - firstly, you should get an email to
the email address registered as an Admin in
your BM - but in your profile, ensure you’ve
turned email alerts on!
You’ll also see a notice when you try to
access Ads Manager regarding Business
Manager status as an alert bar at the top.
Here are the steps we recommend to secure
your Business Manager:
SECTION 1)
Lock Down Your Business Manager
Create two Business Managers
as a backup
You can create two BMs per personal profile
(at the time of writing). Then, when you
create an ad account in one BM, you can
share the ‘assets’ (pixel, audience) to the
second ad account in the separate BM as
a back up.
This is NOT an excuse to do shady stuff! This
is purely in case something goes wrong but
stay on the right side of Facebook compliance
if you care for the longevity of your business
on Facebook.
Activate 2-Factor Authentication (2FA)
on your business manager
This added security ensures you have a lower
chance of being hacked and the hacker using your
account and BM for actions that go against policy,
causing profile, ad account, and Business Manager
loss.
Oh and also avoiding a hacker logging into your ad
account and draining your ad spend by running
illegal ads.
Ensure everyone accessing your BM has 2FA setup.
Whenever Facebook sees something suspicious
they’ll message youwith details. It’s a great feature
and something all advertisers need to be using.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 07
Remove admin and employees anyone not working with your business anymore
It’s important to remember and revoke access from any former employees and ensure admin rights are
only given to the right people else their mistakes might affect the whole Business Manager. Also, people
that have left and then collect bad markers against their profiles (eg going ‘black hat’ and doing things
against policy) this can impact your Business Manager negatively too.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Ensure your Business Manager always has at least 2 admins
Business Manager scores increase when there are more than 2 admins as it shows you have an operating
team. It also means if one admin’s personal profile is banned, you maintain access through the other
admin’s login.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 08
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
By default you can create up to 5 ad accounts within your
Business Manager. Why would you need so many? We’ll
explain below.
For now, it’s important to stress how to build a backup in
case anything goes wrong (which it shouldn’t, because you
have this checklist!).
Ad accounts do get banned accidentally, or for minor policy
violations and can be recovered quickly. It’s good practice to
think and plan ahead.
SECTION 2)
Keep Your Ad Accounts Active
Ensure billing is setup for auto payment, on time
Facebook looks at billing history to see whether all
transactions are coming through on time and continue to be
paid. If your ad account is new, we would also recommend
manually generating and paying invoices once you start
spending money. Pay them bills off quickly!
You can play around with billing thresholds as you mature
the ad account and Facebook trusts you to pay on time.
Create a backup ad account
You never know when you might face policy issues thus we
recommend having one clean account that contains your
pixel only and is used for ‘page boosting’ (running ads
against your Page posts, from $1 per day).
The other ad account is your main ad account for advertising,
into which you share the pixel from your ‘safe’ ad account. If
your main ad account is banned and if it doesn’t impact your
ability to use your BM or personal profile, you can start a new
ad account and share the pixel to the new account.
Appeal or delete existing disapproved ads
A high number of disapproved ads (or disabled ad accounts)
puts your whole Business Manager at risk. A high number of
disabled ads can lead to your Ad Account being banned too
so take this seriously!
www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 09
We use Supermetrics to pull disapproved ads into a Google sheet on a regular basis so we have a
continuous view of ads that need our attention. Watch this YouTube video we created on how to connect
data from Facebook into Google Sheets.
If appealing disapproved ads don’t bring positive results, we recommend deleting disapproved ads which
can turn from ‘mildly’ against policy one week to becoming a reason to completely ban your
ad account the next.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Weekly ‘account quality’ check
Keep a close track of https://business.facebook.com/accountquality/ for your ad accounts to ensure you
keep on top of any issues.
Facebook are aware how much better they need to get at informing you of policy issues and this tool is a
good way of tracking ongoing issues.
Ensure all ad accounts in a BM are active, or delete them
Facebook closes down ad accounts with no activity over a certain period. This counts against you in your
Business Manager.
Use them, or delete them.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 10
You cannot advertise without a Facebook
Page. You might think, well, if I lose my Page
I can at least get a new one for my ad
account, but think about all those fans you’ll
lose and the name too.
So this section is about ensuring your Page
isn’t unpublished for doing things which are
against Facebook’s ads policy etc.
SECTION 3)
Your Page Is As Important As
Anything Else, Don’t Neglect It
Avoid being a direct Admin/Editor on
Pages/Accounts you don’t own
If you are on pages you don’t need to be on,
it’s recommended to remove yourself from
those as Account/Page bans on those can
impact your personal profile, potentially
stopping you from running your own ads.
Keep your monthly feedback score higher
than 3 at all times
If your feedback score drops to 2 or below your
page will be penalized; if your score drops to one
or below your Page will be unpublished and
potentially your advertising account will be
disabled. You can check your feedback score here.
On the next page is an example of a client we took
on that had experienced a Page penalty.
The issue for them was scaling too fast and not
fulfilling their products fast enough. It’s a common
mistake but can prove so costly with Facebook ads.
Their customers voted and made it clear they were
not happy which resulted in them not being able to
use that Page.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Maintain a high positive engagement
rate on your ads, engage with fans,
post new content to your page
Facebook cares about user experience, ads
with low and negative engagement not only
increases your costs but also a risk to get
your ad account disabled.
Keep a keen lookout for ads with a high
negative feedback score - this is a surefire
way of getting your Account banned.
On the right is an example of a Page post
with good engagement. You’re looking for the
section bottom left labelled ‘Negative
Feedback’. You can access this via Ads
Manager within the Page Posts section.
Page post with good engagement
www.getwsodo.com

The issue for them was scaling too fast and not fulfilling their products fast enough. It’s a common mistake
but can prove so costly with Facebook ads. Their customers voted and made it clear they were not happy
which resulted in them not being able to use that Page.
Here’s an example of the best client result we’ve ever seen - they generate around $500k in monthly
Ecommerce revenue and a high number of transactions.
They truly care about their customers and is a strong reflecting of the brand the owners have created.
Their customers are delighted as a result, they have high retention rates and as you can see below, their
Page scores are continuously high.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
But this is more than just keeping Facebook from banning you from advertising - Facebook rewards you in
the auction with the best users when your score is high too.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 12
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Want to know what’s WAY worse than losing BM, Ad
Account or Page? Losing your PERSONAL profile!
Facebook are clamping down more than ever on profiles so
ensure you keep your personal profile safe! etc
SECTION 4)
You Cannot Advertise
Without a Personal Profile,
So Keep It Protected
Don’t share your login details with anyone
Any logins from unknown devices/locations can trigger not
only a profile to be locked but also affect your advertising
accounts.
We’ve seen people sharing passwords with virtual assistants
or team members. This introduces the risk of Facebook
flagging your personal profile as being hacked - potentially
seeing you login from the US then within a few hours seeing
you login in the Far East. This can lead to security challenges
and potential profile bans.
Check-in on your newsfeed on your personal
account when entering a new country
This is a little known hack that shows Facebook you are
actually changing locations and decreases a chance to get
locked out of your account on security grounds.
The logic is that as long as your mobile device has GPS
enabled, Facebook can tell where you are. But we go a step
further and use the check-in feature.
Don’t use your personal profile for business
purposes
We’ve seen many users since 2019 getting hit by this quite
badly with some well known names losing their personal
profiles. Whilst it’s generally ok to talk about your business,
adding links, offers, products and services with links out from
your personal newsfeed is a risk not worth taking.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 13
Use 2 factor authentication for your personal profile
Facebook will alert you if there’s an unauthorised login and using a random code generator which you
can install on your mobile can ensure you keep yourself Facebook and increase Facebook’s trust for
your profile.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
The Messenger alerts are definitely a great addition to keep an eye on who’s trying to log in to your profile
and from where.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 14
SECTION 5)
Landing Pages Must Remain Compliant Too,
They’re Part Of The User Experience
Bonus: load your pixel through a tagging tool
like Google Tag Manager to take advantage of
‘async’ loading - which means your pixel should
not be delayed from loading behind your web
content.
This is possibly the most overlooked of all
compliance issues - your landing page IS part
of your user experience (in fact your whole
sales funnel).
The most common reason for the ‘poor user
experience’ tag used by Facebook in some ad
disapprovals is often down to your landing
page. So make sure you’re keeping your
entire user experience in good shape - and
Facebook will reward you with ads that run
and, if you’re really good, lower costs and
higher quality users too.
We call that a WIN:WIN:WIN for you, users
and Facebook!
Avoid single page sites with no links
This is a ‘poor user experience’ and in many
cases your ad will be disapproved. This is
because Facebook doesn't want you to create
a user experience which is a dead-end.
Even if you have a call to action which directs
them to call a number they still expect some bare
minimum links like Policy, Privacy and Contact Us.
For a better user experience ensure
your landing page is fast and content
is closely related to your ads
Facebook not only cares about the user
experience on their platform but also outside
of it, including your landing pages.
How does Facebook know how fast your
page loads? Well, you have the pixel installed
right? Facebook sees everything on your
website through that.
How does Facebook know if your landing
page content and ads align? They also track
‘bounces’ - that is, someone that clicks an ad,
lands on your website and hits that back
button to exit within 10 seconds. Not a good
sign for you.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Tip: Go check out your page load times on a
website like GTMetrix or Pingdom and
ensure your page is loading fast relative to
what’s on it. For Ecommerce a page load
time of 2-3 seconds is good, slightly longer
for info product sales pages.
Follow the advice on those speed test websites on
ways to optimise speed.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 15
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Additional Suggestions To Keep Your Ad
Account Active
Error pages or sites that are under
construction(don’t install the pixel until it is
ready!)
Landing pages that are PDF or JPEG files
Landing pages that don't match the product or
service promoted in the ad
Landing pages that require you to download
additional programs or software to access the
content on the page
Landing pages with a low Web of Trust rating
Directing people to a closed or secret Facebook
group from an ad
Sending people to www.facebook.com
Landing pages that automatically download files
to a person's computer
Avoid directing ads to landing pages with
minimal original content, or content that is
difficult to access
Make sure your landing page's original content is
not obstructed by ads or by links to other content
The original content on your landing page should
be clear and easily navigable
Avoid distractions from your landing page's
original content, such as excessive pop-up ads,
scrolling, clicks…
Avoid baiting people to click, such as overly
cropped image ads
The content & branding on your landing page
must be relevant to your ad
Avoid interstitial pages, advertorials and similar
that act as bounce pages from ad to the main
site (perceived by Facebook ads low value)
Avoid the following:
Verify your URL
This helps build trust that you as the ad
account owner are also the owner of the
website (as opposed to a reseller or affiliate).
More info here on how to take advantage of
this.
Trust the domain you’re working
with
Facebook works with Web of Trust to verify
domains and keep users safe. If working with
a client or as an affiliate for example, check
the URL is safe. If for any reason it has been
marked as unsafe, your ads will likely get
disapproved and often people don’t know
why.
Ensure your landing page
is functional
Ads must not direct people to non-functional
landing pages. This includes landing page
content that “interferes with a person’s ability
to navigate away from the page” such as full-
screen email capture tools and offers.
Fake the scarcity at your own risk
Scarcity timers for your Ecommerce and Lead
Gen offers might work for some customers
and in the right context, are acceptable, but
creating fake scarcity is something Facebook
dislikes and can lead to ad disapprovals.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 16
Ok this is definitely the hardest of all. Keeping
ads compliant is a full-time hobby for us
because we like to keep our accounts running
smoothly.
So whilst the earlier parts of this guide you’ll
check less frequently, we expect you’ll be
checking your ads for compliance most times
you create an ad (and definitely re-checking
once you hit the inevitable disapproval).
Firstly, it’s important to note that most ads go
through an automated review process, with
trigger words based on some of the items
below.
For example we run ads to our Info Products
and are often hit with the generic ‘MLM’
warning when we’re not building any kind of
MLM program.
In this instance we’d usually just appeal
with a message to this effect and, if we’re
fortunate we’ll get a manual review and
approval.
But at times you’ll hit the automation again
and may end up with a permanent ad
disapproval. It’s ok, recreate it via duplication,
make any necessary edits and delete the
disapproved ad.
Here are the most common reasons for ads
being disapproved from our experience.
Facebook’s policy website has the full list.
SECTION 6)
Start Your Ads Compliant And Keep Them That Way!
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Be truthful and clear in your copy
Avoid false, misleading, fraudulent, or
deceptive claims. If you do have a claim
then ensure it can be backed up and you’re
representing it clearly.
“Speak 12 languages in 20 days”
“Lose 15 lbs. in 7 days”
“Earn X billion dollars in a couple of days”
Not Allowed:
Non-numeric results in a specific time frame
“Learn languages in 5 days”
“Build programming skills in 3 months”
“Learn how to bike-ride in a week!”
A Non-Health and Non-Employment
claim or testimonial with a Disclaimer
“Speak 12 languages in 20 days.
*Results may vary.”
Allowed:
www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 17
Withholding
“There are foods that are bad for
Arthritis. These are the worst 5.”
Sensationalism
“7 MIND-BLOWING uses of carrots!”
“These diets will blow your mind!”
“All jazz fans must check this out!”
Engagement Bait
“LIKE this or LOVE this if you love puppies”
“SHARE this with your friend
that loves jazz music”
“Respond with 'YES' if you agree”
“Tag your friend that loves puppies too”
Not Allowed:
Avoid tricking users to click your ad
As tempting as it might be, if and when Facebook finds your ad they’ll disapprove it and, since this is used
so widely by black hat advertisers, you could get your Account banned too.
Don’t hold back on information in your ad to try and get the user to click and avoid anything that
sensationalises your ad too.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Tag Baiting 	Comment Baiting
HERE ARE A FEW EXAMPLES FROM FACEBOOK:
If running ads in the Health niche, be careful about the images you use and the
messages you’re implying
The challenge here is that Facebook doesn't want a negative experience in the newsfeed. I recall an ad a
client ran on a new skincare regime showing nasty looking images of a woman’s arm.
If something is shocking, or not so nice to look at in your ad, users will complain and blame Facebook. So
this is why Facebook are so harsh on us as advertisers.
The most common thing to avoid is ‘before’ and ‘after’ ads - this mainly affects the health and beauty
niche because of a) the problem of people photoshopping in fake results and b) that this implies
something which may not have been explained properly.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 18
1) Close-ups on body parts or organs
2) Tape measures/ scale
3) A person with ill-fitting clothes
4) Person pinching their fat/cellulite
5) Before and After images
6) Images that may shock or scare viewers
7) Images that are scary, gory or sensational
8) Avoid using images with something
being penetrated inside the body like syringes
9) Person Ads depicting violence or threats
of violence
Not Allowed:
1) A general focus on health
2) Full/ un-zoomed body images
3) Focusing on physical fitness
Allowed:
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
IMAGE 	PERMITED?
Here are a few examples from Facebook:
Images including full or non-zoomed figures
A general focus on personal health
www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 19

Ad images focusing on zoomed body parts
Ad imaging focusing on

Ad image focusing on weight loss

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 20
Focusing on before and after
This image shows a gun
pointing toward the viewer and
is non-compliant
This image shows penetration
and is thus non-compliant
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
IMAGE 	PERMITED?
If promoting adult services, or apparel with a lot of ‘skin’ be careful about how you
pitch it
Adult products and services must focus on features and NOT the sizzle! This goes against what we
generally teach, which is to share the emotional or sizzle of what you’re selling!
But in this case, you need to keep it clean and also ensure you’re targeting people 18 or older too.
As for those selling things like swimwear where your ads may contain flesh, ensure the images are non-
sexual and non-suggestive.
www.getwsodo.com

“Condoms to enhance your pleasure."
“Use our gel to dramatically improve your
sex life.”
“Buy our sex toys for your adult pleasure.”
Sex toys or products focused explicitly on
sexual pleasure.
Nudity or implied nudity
Excessive visible skin or cleavage, even if
not explicitly sexual in nature
Images focused on individual body parts,
such as abs, buttocks or chest, even if not
explicitly sexual in nature
Dating ads where the focus of the ad is on
a partially clothed model
Content portraying excessive nudity or
alluding to sexual activity
Not Allowed:
“Free condoms at your local student
health center." This example text is
compliant, as long as it's targeted to
people 18 years or older
“Practice safe sex with our brand of
condoms.” This text is compliant, as long
as it's targeted to people 18 years or
older
Allowed:
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Avoid calling out personal attributes or implying that you know something about the
user (even if you do!)
It’s easy to get this one wrong and again it falls into the area of avoiding making users feel uncomfortable.
When reviewing your ad, think about it would make you feel if you saw the ad and avoid calling anything
out which would make them annoyed that Facebook has shared this with you, the advertiser (whether
they did or not).
Text referencing or alluding to personal
attributes or characteristics of the targeted
group or individual
Using the word "other" to reference a
personal characteristic
Not Allowed:
Ad creative that describes or shows the
promoted product or service
Allowed:

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 23
The process to review could take minutes,
hours or days depending on whether it's
an ad, account or Business Manager issue.
The more ads that get disapproved, the
higher chance of an account ban
Red flags can be placed on your Business
Manager and any assets related to it
including payment methods, website
URLs, IP addresses and more. This is to
ensure bad actors cannot simply recreate
assets without proper compliance.
Plan campaigns in advance to ensure any
ad disapprovals can be caught early
Some assets are automatically
reviewed/acted upon by machines and
others go through a manual review.
Chances are the longer it takes the more
likely it is to be a manual review
A Facebook Partner Manager may help to
expedite issues depending on your
relationship, but equally and often,
Facebook’s business chat can help resolve
issues fast.
Be open and honest with Facebook’s policy
team, ensuring you know what mistakes
you’ve made and that they won’t be repeated.
It might feel like you’re apologising to your
parents or partner for being naughty but it’s
the only way.
Few notes to consider:
Ok, You Either Got Unlucky Or You Didn’t Get This Guide In
Time And Lost Your Ad Account or Business Manager -
NOW WHAT?
Appeal ad disapprovals
Firstly, if an ad is disapproved after you
publish, before you try to appeal ensure it is
compliant. If not, it could and likely will get
permanently disapproved.
If you want to bulk request approval (without
individually clicking each ad in Ads Manager)
then use this link:
https://www.facebook.com/help/contact/1582364
792025146
If after appealing, your ad is permanently rejected,
you can either make an edit and try publishing again
or make a copy and submit it again.
In cases where you’re confident it passes then try
this. However, do ensure you fix policy issues
before submitting so you don’t waste your own
time and you avoid picking up negativity on your ad
account.
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 24
Appeal an Ad Account ban
Ad account ban appeals can be fairly successful but it depends on how severe the penalty was before
hand.
At the time of writing Facebook are developing tools to help us better track and understand this but much
of this is detective work from you, which is why people hire us to do this work for them.
You’re not expected to know and understand all the policies which is where we come in and advise on
how best to recover.
We understand you can appeal up to 3 times so make it count.
To try get your ad account back yourself you will need to appeal the decision using this URL:
https://www.facebook.com/help/contact/2026068680760273 and follow the script below:
1) Introduce yourself, your business (how long its been running, your niche, and anything to support how
legitimate your business is)
2) Apologize for any known issues or misunderstandings
3) Let them know exactly why your ad account was banned, which policies it violated (contact us if you
need help with this)
4) If possible, present ad ID’s, campaign ID’s or similar where policy violations may have occurred
5) Explain you’ve learned from your mistake(s) or misunderstanding(s) and have a fix in place for when the
ban is lifted (it helps to mention that you’ve reviewed Facebook’s compliance documentation and better
understand how to remain compliant)
6) Ask what other information you could provide to the policy team to help solve this issue (sometimes
they’ll need ID or business papers for you to verify that your business is legit).
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Appeal a Business Manager ban
Well, firstly, whilst a tiny number of Business
Manager bans are a mistake, the majority are
because of very bad policy based penalties. This
is the ultimate punishment.
Our experience with trying to overturn Business
Manager bans is a simple case of if the penalty
was severe, close to no chance otherwise the
chance rests on how well we can build a case.
We generally recommend following the same
process as an account ban, except you use this
following link:
https://www.facebook.com/help/contact/21661
73276743732
www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 28 --

WWW.SMCOMMERCE.CO.UK | 25
• 	F A C E B O O K 	A D S 	C A M P A I G N 	L A U N C H 	C O M P L I A N C E 	C H E C K L I S T 	•
Start over
Depending on the ban type, you might be unable to
recover your accounts, especially if multiple policies
were breached. If you start a new ad account, you
might need to also add a new pixel to your site, if the
banned ad account contained your pixel.
You can by default (at time of writing) create up to 5 ad
accounts per Business Manager. More can be created
once your Business Manager has matured and/or your
spend has increased and your account is in good
standing, through chat support or your Facebook rep.
If you do decide to start a new business & have
permanently banned ad accounts/business managers
below are some recommendations when starting an
entirely new account.
1) Using a new, however, aged & verified domain name
2) Using only new creatives/ad copies
3) Using a new profile, machine, IP address
4) START SLOW, begin by increasing likes on your
new page
www.getwsodo.com

ADDITIONAL RESOURCES
This document is intended as a reference guide to help you avoid issues and overcome
any that do appear
It’s a part of our Facebook Ads Management Framework to ensure accounts and ads remain compliant
with Facebook’s policies, which change very often.
Even though we try to geek out at keeping up to date with Facebook Compliance - because let's face it,
you’ll make more money with a running ad account and ads than you can with one that’s banned! - we still
sometimes hit issues.
We work very closely with Facebook and the Policy team to try and keep our understanding of Policy up
to date.
Prevention is 100% better than cure!
We truly encourage everyone to try and stay compliant. Prevention is so much better than cure - we
receive weekly requests to help overturn bans and disapprovals and whilst sometimes we win, often times
we don’t.
Once Facebook has flagged any part of your advertising it can be a downward spiral.
The worst thing to happen to a successful ad account is to hit any kind of policy issue.
Imagine you’re scaling, using all the right strategies, tactics, growing, hiring etc and suddenly you’re hit
with the dreaded ban.
It’s crippling, much like a natural disaster wiping out a bricks and mortar store, a big enough policy issue
can wipe out the majority of your traffic.
It’s the reason we created this guide for our internal team, for clients and have now published it for you,
too.
www.getwsodo.com
